title: Troubleshooting Guide
description: Solutions for common DemiEngine errors and issues

sections.title: Troubleshooting Overview
sections.content: This guide helps you diagnose and fix common issues when using DemiEngine. Most problems stem from incorrect hex formatting, uninitialized registers, or logic errors in program flow. The debug GUI is your best tool for understanding execution.

sections.title: Invalid Instruction Error
sections.content:
**Symptom:** Program halts with "Invalid instruction" error

**Causes:**
- Incorrect opcode in hex program
- Jumping to data section or uninitialized memory
- Corrupted program file

**Solution:**
```hex
# Bad: Invalid opcode
FE                  # Invalid opcode (not defined)

# Good: Valid opcode
FF                  # HALT (valid)
```

**Debug Steps:**
1. Check the address where error occurred
2. Verify opcodes against instruction set reference
3. Ensure program counter isn't jumping to data

sections.title: Memory Access Violation
sections.content:
**Symptom:** "Memory access violation" or segmentation fault

**Causes:**
- Accessing memory outside allocated range
- Uninitialized address registers
- Stack overflow

**Solution:**
```hex
# Bad: Uninitialized register
06 00 01            # LOAD R0, [R1]  ; R1 not initialized!

# Good: Initialize first
01 01 10 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R1, 0x10
06 00 01            # LOAD R0, [R1]   ; Now safe
```

**Prevention:**
- Always initialize address registers before use
- Check array bounds before access
- Monitor stack pointer in recursive functions

sections.title: Stack Overflow/Underflow
sections.content:
**Symptom:** Stack-related errors or unexpected behavior

**Causes:**
- Infinite recursion
- Too many PUSH without POP
- POP from empty stack

**Solution:**
```hex
# Bad: Infinite recursion
my_function:
0C 00 00 00 00      # CALL my_function  ; Calls itself!

# Good: Base case
my_function:
0E 00 01            # CMP R0, R1        ; Check condition
0F 10 00 00 00      # JZ base_case      ; Exit condition
0C 00 00 00 00      # CALL my_function  ; Recursive call
base_case:
0D                  # RET
```

sections.title: Division by Zero
sections.content:
**Symptom:** Program halts on DIV instruction

**Cause:** Attempting to divide by zero

**Solution:**
```hex
# Good: Check before division
0E 01 02            # CMP R1, R2        ; Check if divisor is 0
0F 20 00 00 00      # JZ error_handler  ; Handle zero case
12 00 01            # DIV R0, R1        ; Safe division

error_handler:
# Handle division by zero appropriately
```

sections.title: Build and Compilation Problems
sections.content:
**Missing Dependencies:**
```bash
# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt update
sudo apt install g++ libfmt-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libglfw3-dev
```

**C++17 Support:**
```bash
# Check compiler version
g++ --version

# Update if needed (Ubuntu)
sudo apt install g++-9
```

**Linking Errors:**
```bash
# Verify libraries
pkg-config --cflags --libs fmt
pkg-config --cflags --libs glfw3
```

**Clean Build:**
```bash
make clean
make
```

sections.title: Runtime Problems
sections.content:
**File Not Found:**
```bash
# Check file path and permissions
ls -la tests/your_program.hex
./bin/demi-engine tests/your_program.hex

# Use absolute path
./bin/demi-engine $(pwd)/tests/your_program.hex
```

**Permission Issues:**
```bash
# Make executable
chmod +x bin/demi-engine

# Verify permissions
ls -la bin/demi-engine
```

**GUI Won't Start:**
```bash
# Check display
echo $DISPLAY

# SSH X11 forwarding
ssh -X username@hostname

# Check OpenGL
glxinfo | grep "OpenGL version"
```

sections.title: Using Debug GUI
sections.content:
**Enable GUI:**
```bash
./bin/demi-engine your_program.hex --gui
```

**Key Debug Windows:**
- **CPU State:** Monitor registers and flags
- **Memory View:** Examine memory contents
- **Device Status:** Check device states

**Step-by-Step Execution:**
1. Load program in GUI
2. Set breakpoints at key instructions
3. Step through execution
4. Watch register and memory changes

sections.title: Manual Debugging Techniques
sections.content:
**Add Debug Output:**
```hex
# Print debug messages
01 00 XX 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R0, debug_message_addr
39 01 00            # OUTSTR port=1, R0
```

**Register Dumps:**
```hex
# Output register values
02 00 01            # ADD R0, R1    ; R1 = ASCII '0'
31 01 00            # OUT port=1, R0 ; Print R0 as digit
```

**Enable Logging:**
```bash
# Run with debug output
./bin/demi-engine -d program.hex 2>&1 | tee debug.log
```

sections.title: Infinite Loops
sections.content:
**Symptom:** Program never terminates

**Cause:** Loop without exit condition

**Solution:**
```hex
# Bad: No exit condition
loop_start:
07 00 00 00 00      # JMP loop_start  ; Infinite loop!

# Good: Proper loop control
01 00 0A 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R0, 10   ; Counter
loop_start:
13 00               # DEC R0            ; Decrement
0E 00 02            # CMP R0, R2        ; Compare with 0
08 XX XX XX XX      # JNZ loop_start    ; Continue if not zero
```

sections.title: Hex Format Errors
sections.content:
**Invalid Characters:**
```hex
# Bad: Invalid characters
01 0G 10 00 00 00    # 'G' is not valid hex

# Good: Valid hex only (0-9, A-F)
01 06 10 00 00 00    # Correct format
```

**Missing Bytes:**
```hex
# Bad: Incomplete instruction
01 00               # LOAD_IMM needs 6 bytes total

# Good: Complete instruction
01 00 10 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R0, 0x10 (6 bytes)
```

**Tips:**
- Each hex digit represents 4 bits
- Instructions must be complete (check opcode reference)
- Use spaces or newlines for readability

sections.title: Register Corruption
sections.content:
**Problem:** Overwriting important values

**Solution:**
```hex
# Bad: Overwriting register
01 00 10 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R0, 16
# ... later in code ...
01 00 05 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R0, 5  ; Overwrites R0!

# Good: Save/restore or use different register
01 00 10 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R0, 16
0A 00               # PUSH R0          ; Save value
01 00 05 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R0, 5   ; Temporary use
# ... use R0 ...
0B 00               # POP R0           ; Restore value
```

**Best Practices:**
- Save important registers before function calls
- Use R0-R7 for general purposes
- Document register usage in comments

sections.title: Control Flow Errors
sections.content:
**Missing Return:**
```hex
# Bad: Function falls through
my_function:
02 00 01            # ADD R0, R1
# Missing RET!

# Good: Always return
my_function:
02 00 01            # ADD R0, R1
1B                  # RET
```

**Wrong Jump Addresses:**
```hex
# Use comments to mark addresses

# Address 0x10:
target_address:
01 00 05 00 00 00    # LOAD_IMM R0, 5

# Jump to 0x10
05 10 00 00 00      # JMP target_address
```

**Tips:**
- Calculate addresses carefully
- Use assembler labels when possible
- Document jump targets

sections.title: String Handling Errors
sections.content:
**Missing Null Terminators:**
```hex
# Bad: String without null
48 65 6C 6C 6F      # "Hello" - missing \0

# Good: Null-terminated
48 65 6C 6C 6F 00    # "Hello\0"
```

**Buffer Overflows:**
- Allocate sufficient buffer space
- Check string lengths before copying
- Always null-terminate strings

**OUTSTR Requirements:**
- String must be null-terminated
- R0 must contain valid memory address
- Port must be configured (usually port 1)

sections.title: Device Communication Problems
sections.content:
**Console Not Responding:**
1. Check console device registered at port 1
2. Verify OUTSTR/INSTR instructions correct
3. Ensure strings null-terminated
4. Check if device initialized

**File Device Issues:**
1. Verify file permissions and paths
2. Ensure file exists and is readable
3. Check device properly initialized
4. Review device port configuration

**Debug Steps:**
```bash
# Enable device logging
./bin/demi-engine -d -v program.hex
```

sections.title: Performance Issues
sections.content:
**Slow Execution:**
- Check for infinite loops (no exit condition)
- Optimize tight loops (minimize memory access)
- Cache frequently used values in registers
- Use better algorithms

**Memory Usage:**
- Optimize data structures
- Remove unused code and data
- Increase memory size if needed

**Profiling:**
```bash
# Use time command
time ./bin/demi-engine program.hex

# Enable debug timing
./bin/demi-engine -d program.hex | grep "Execution time"
```

sections.title: Getting Help
sections.content:
**When Reporting Issues:**

1. **Complete Program:** Provide full code that reproduces issue
2. **Error Messages:** Include exact error text
3. **Platform Info:** OS, compiler version
4. **Steps to Reproduce:** Clear sequence of commands

**Debug Information:**
```bash
# Compile with debug symbols
make debug

# Run with full logging
./bin/demi-engine -d -v program.hex 2>&1 | tee debug.log
```

**Community Resources:**
- Example programs in `examples/` directory
- Documentation in `docs/` folder
- Debug GUI for visual inspection
- Start simple and build complexity gradually
